text.title: LB Layer Selection Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for L4 vs L7 selection. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Choose L4 ONLY when traffic is pure TCP / UDP without content-routing needs (gaming, streaming, database proxies, raw Redis). L4 must NOT be selected for HTTP workloads that need URL/path routing.
rule.rationale: L4 has near-zero per-packet inspection cost but cannot read HTTP. Forcing L4 onto HTTP workloads with content routing requires hacks (separate ports per path) that break basic L7 features.

rule.statement: Pick L7 when ANY of: content-based routing (path, header, host, cookie); SSL termination at the LB; HTTP header manipulation; WAF integration; API-gateway features.

rationale.source: Cloudflare LB learning hub + Mozilla guidelines

rule.rationale: These features all require parsing HTTP — only L7 can express them. Workarounds at L4 (DNS sharding, port-per-path) collapse into operational debt.

rule.statement: If TLS termination at the LB is required, the LB MUST operate at L7. L4 pass-through ends TLS at the backend; L4 termination is only possible with a private-key on the L4 box (rare, increases blast radius).

rationale.source: Mozilla TLS guidelines + cloud LB references

rule.rationale: Mixing "L4" with "terminate TLS here" usually means the agent really wants L7 — confront the inconsistency rather than build something exotic.

rule.statement: For anycast or cross-zone IP-level distribution, layer an L4 LB (AWS NLB, GCP Network LB, HAProxy TCP mode) IN FRONT of an L7 LB (ALB, Ingress-Nginx, Envoy). Treat them as two distinct tiers, not a single binary choice.

rationale.source: AWS Global Accelerator + GCP LB references

rule.rationale: L4 owns anycast + connection multiplexing; L7 owns content routing. Collapsing both responsibilities into one product is rarely optimal.

rule.statement: Reject any architecture proposal that names "L4" but lists L7 features (URL routing, header rewrite, WAF, cookie persistence). Either upgrade the choice to L7 or split into hybrid L4-front-of-L7.
rule.rationale: This contradiction is the single most common LLM failure on LB design. Catching it at review time avoids 6-month rebuilds.
